title: I Got Cornered on TV by 6 Doctors

chunks.title: Introduction to Carnivore Diet Experience
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino shares his experience on a TV show where he defended the carnivore diet against multiple opposing doctors and a lawyer. He discusses how the carnivore diet has helped individuals with lupus-like symptoms.
chunks.transcript: [Applause] Dr saladino when you're recommending this diet what risks do you discuss with your patients oh my God guys I get this this video triggers me so let's see where this goes the backstory here I was invited on the doctor's TV show in 2019 I went on with my friends Sarah and Ashley Olen who had significantly improved their lupus like symptoms with a strictly carnivore diet I went to Los Angeles I went on the show and before the show the producer comes in and says oh do you mind if we have it be sort of a debate okay I'm fine with that imagining that it's going to be a real debate where I get to talk and we actually discuss science I gave the producers and the other doctors on the show a list of 22 references that I would be discussing in the debate little did I know it was not going to be a real debate and right before they show they said oh do you mind if we add another doctor uh a vegan doctor and I said fine whatever so it ended up being five or six doctors against me was five doctors plus a lawyer as we'll see against me I don't think there are any risks to eating animal Foods exclusively there are no risks in terms of kidney function there are many studies which show that high protein diets do not affect kidney function there are no studies interventionally that show that eating meat is going to increase the risk of any single cancer there are no risks to eating I love at that point in the video they have somebody behind me just shaking her head as I'm saying there are no risks of eating a significant amount of meat on kidney function there's no real Interventional studies that show that eating meat increases your risk of cancer etc etc obviously since this uh video was taken I've changed my perspective a little bit on what I eat I now eat some fruit some honey some raw milk if you guys know about an animal- based diet but uh I still stand by the fact that meat and organs are incredibly healthy for humans there are no risks to eating the foods that we have been eating as humans for our entire

chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino emphasizes his expertise and the interruptions he faced during the debate. He explains his medical training and the significant self-education he pursued to understand nutrition better.

chunks.title: Critique of Medical Nutrition Knowledge
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino critiques the lack of nutrition education in medical schools, stating that many doctors don't have comprehensive training in nutrition. He argues that anyone can become knowledgeable through independent research, regardless of formal degrees.

chunks.title: Defense of the Carnivore Diet
chunks.summary: In this segment, Dr. Saladino defends the benefits of the carnivore diet against critiques, suggesting there are no inherent risks in eating meat exclusively. He references historical studies supporting the carnivore diet.

chunks.title: Controversy Over Nutrient Claims
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino addresses claims made by opposing doctors that meat lacks essential vitamins like vitamin C and K. He argues that meat does contain these nutrients and suggests that the criticisms stem from a misunderstanding of nutritional science.

chunks.title: Discussing Plant-Based Foods and Their Effects
chunks.summary: Dr. Saladino shares insights on how certain commonly considered healthy foods, like vegetables and grains, can negatively impact health, especially for those with autoimmune issues. He discusses the potential pitfalls of consuming these foods.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Reflection on TV Appearance
chunks.summary: Reflecting on his TV debate experience, Dr. Saladino describes the emotional aftermath and the positive impact he believes it has had in promoting discussion around animal-based diets. He invites those from the show to join him on his podcast for further discussion.
